label: agentdb • reference flow: seed yesterday, import today, trend, deliver
query: You are a daily GitHub analyst. Your agent database already holds yesterday's star snapshot in a table repo_stats(day, repo, stars). Today's snapshot is in the file stars_today.csv in your working folder (columns: day, repo, stars). Do the following: (1) import stars_today.csv into the repo_stats table; (2) define a saved view named star_trend that returns each day with the total stars across all repos as columns (day, total), ordered by day, and run it; (3) share a concise markdown artifact named star_trend.md that states today's total stars and how it changed versus yesterday.
notes: End-to-end reference flow for the Agent DB superpowers, built from EXISTING tools: db_import (acquire today's file) -> db_define_view + db_run_view (SQL trend) -> share_artifact (deliver). seedSql stages yesterday (total 150) into a table shaped like a real db_create_table table (id PK + _created_at/_updated_at/_deleted_at) so db_import/db_schema compose with it the way they would on a table the agent created on day 1 — a bare `CREATE TABLE` would lack the system columns the typed tools stamp/read. The today fixture adds 130+45=175 (delta +25). dbState verifies the numbers against the base table (saved views are stored definitions, not real SQL views, so the assertions don't depend on the view object). mustCallToolsInOrder pins acquire -> derive -> deliver; mustNotCallTools forbids the per-row db_insert fallback. selfScheduling is enabled so the model MAY also notify, but only share_artifact is required.

seedSql: CREATE TABLE repo_stats (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, day TEXT, repo TEXT, stars INTEGER, _created_at INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%s','now')), _updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%s','now')), _deleted_at INTEGER);
INSERT INTO repo_stats (day, repo, stars) VALUES ('2026-06-20','repoA',100),('2026-06-20','repoB',50);

dbState.sql: SELECT SUM(stars) FROM repo_stats WHERE day = '2026-06-21'
SELECT day, SUM(stars) AS total FROM repo_stats GROUP BY day ORDER BY day
